author: @excitedbox5705
text: Quite short video and not much info about how it ACTUALLY works. Universal motors NEED a controller. They will keep accelerating until they fail otherwise. It seems this channel has just been rapid firing short videos without any kind of theme or really completing anything. It would be much nicer to see a few 20 minute videos that take a project from start to finish instead of this ADHD of random.

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Luke, how many amps at 12 volts are required to run the pressure washer at close to the same pressure as it ran on AC power. I was just wondering how you would go about making a cordless pressure washer and how long a 100 Ah 12 volt AGM sealed battery might last. Being a sealed AGM battery it wouldn't matter if it got a bit wet so long as the terminals were waterproofed to avoid the possibility of arcing.
Cool video mate.

author: @enigma_7
text: Ive never taking a pressure washer apart before. You could turn it into a generator and make a gravity generator and use a washing machine pump as the generator. Then use a solar panel to charge a battery to use for resetting the gravity generator (the gravity light).

author: @flytrapjohn
text:
That's a typical swash plate piston pump. Extremely common hydraulic pumps. Some are adjustable by changing the overall depth plate " wobble". 
Same mechanism used in some reciprocating saws. Thanks for letting us see the inside of this little beastie.

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: I have seen some showing water jet cutters they made out of power washers, but also heard that electric paint guns (like those you see running normally off an air compressor to paint walls in a house and such but these just plug into a wall and everything to flow the paint is in the handle on the paint container hanging under it, and should have a warning about not putting your hands in front of the nozzle to see if it is working because it will inject paint into the skin and cause gang green and such problems LOL) anyway the fact that it worked could allow that to  be used as a sand blaster cutting tool too. (there is a video where a guy made one using a tupper ware storage container and some cheap rubber gloves, ect, air compressor and hobby tubing used in model railroad and such stuff, and cut spiral cuts in glass test tubes and such, and I bet a good unit on a laser engraver CNC control could be used to cut things, quite well! Wondering what this could be made to do, if water were used instead of air to blow that sand material into something for cutting action maybe for cutting stone and such, (also air assist with a laser is a thing, that vastly improves cutting capability of even the weakest laser. Found that out myself, but now I am wondering how water could be used instead of air, and jetting the laser into it as well, which I still have to set things up so I can use it in a salt tank to attempt to create voltage differentials enough that the salt can be used to etch metals with the chlorine or sodium, or maybe another salt will work better like magnesium sulfate to get sulfuric acid reactions to cut the metal or something, from the charge of the photons in the metals or what ever charging it to attract one and not the other half of the binary acid base salts. ) or you could use it for all sorts of liquids as opposed to water for who knows what. :) Can't wait to see what you decide to do with it, also (see next comment)

author: @saiello2061
text: Ah, I always imagined the pump to be a single positive displacement one like a gear or vane type. Interesting to see that its effectively like a water equivalent of a 3-phase generator. Does it also have an expansion chamber on the output too, i.e., the water equivalent of an electrical smoothing capacitor...?

replies.author: @deezy_da_M_E
replies.text: Kinda but its more like an ac compressor in a car i think. It could have 3,4,5,6 etc pistons to pump the water, but a pressure washer is more like a booster pump cuz the water will already be pressurized to about 30-40psi from the water main. It would be like a 3 phase gen if you ran all 3 phases together into one, like thru a rectifier or something. Like going from 3 phase ac to dc like the charging system on most motorcycles.

author: @mr.upcycle9589
text: Would have been nice to break the pump down further, to see how the springs interact to increase the pressure. I assume they force the water through a series of one way valves. But hard to know what the actual process is.

replies.author: @erlendse
replies.text:
The springs are clearly for the return of pistons. The disc only pushes, but no pulling.

Still, agree about the system needing more exploration!

author: @edlubs11
text: Can you use the whole unit to make a waterfall piece? It is nice to look at, listen to, and even rejuvenates the water. Look into the work of Victor Shauberger!

author: @MrAnderson4509
text: Luke, that is a cool little beastie, is there a "wobble plate" that works the the springs up and down when driven by the motor? I just Couldn't really see if the plate was pinched or had a cam lobe, in either case, It was cool you pulled it apart for us I have not seen the inside of that design, I'm used to in-line three cylinder in line conventional units that I laid out breadboard style  That rotary design looks more reliable to me

replies.author: @nitrousman8882
replies.text: you missed the part where he mentions that the bearing on the shaft is on an angle.  When Luke says "synchronizes" the pump, I think an easier way to look at it is that the rotating angled plate sequentially compresses the pump pistons, If he tears down the pump portion, we might find that the pistons are progressively smaller, effecting a multi stage compression effect.  Low volume but high pressure :)

replies.author: @MrAnderson4509

author: @bkstump
text: It's not clear how the flat rotating disk can push each of the three spring loaded plungers on the "pump". Cool takedown, though. 🤔👍🏻

replies.author: @stubluesjhb1
replies.text: The disk is mounted on an angle so it pushes on of the spring plungers one at a time.  Freeze frame the video and you can just see it mounted on the angle.
